title: Mildella smithii
intro: Mildella smithii, also known as the 'False Pimenta', is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders and infections. Bioactive constituents of Mildella smithii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Mildella smithii include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to support digestive health and immune function.
taxonomy: Mildella smithii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Mildella. The specific classification of Mildella smithii is within the subfamily Rubioideae. The plant's classification is further detailed at the species level, but the exact classification may require further research. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Mildella smithii shares characteristics with other flowering plants in this family.
